title: The Old Bullock Dray
content:
آ آ آ آ Oh! the shearing is all over,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And the wool is coming down,
آ آ آ آ And I mean to get a wife, boys,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ When I go up to town.
آ آ آ آ Everything that has two legs
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Represents itself in view,
آ آ آ آ From the little paddy-melon
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ To the bucking kangaroo.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ CHORUS

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  So itï؟½s roll up your blankets,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  And letï؟½s make a push,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Iï؟½ll take you up the country,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  And show you the bush.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Iï؟½ll be bound you wonï؟½t get
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Such a chance another day,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  So come and take possession
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Of my old bullock dray.

آ آ آ آ Now, Iï؟½ve saved up a good cheque,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ I mean to buy a team,
آ آ آ آ And when I get a wife, boys,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll be all-serene
آ آ آ آ For calling at the depï؟½t.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ They say thereï؟½s no delay
آ آ آ آ To get an off-sider
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ For the old bullock dray.

آ آ آ آ Oh! weï؟½ll live like fighting cocks,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ For good living, Iï؟½m your man.
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ll have leather jacks, johnny cakes,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And fritters in the pan;
آ آ آ آ Or if youï؟½d like some fish
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll catch you some soon,
آ آ آ آ For weï؟½ll bob for barramundies
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Round the banks of a lagoon.

آ آ آ آ Oh! yes, of beef and damper
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ I take care we have enough,
آ آ آ آ And weï؟½ll boil in the bucket
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Such a whopper of a duff,
آ آ آ آ And our friends will dance
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ To the honour of the day,
آ آ آ آ To the music of the bells,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Around the old bullock dray.

آ آ آ آ Oh! weï؟½ll have plenty girls,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ We must mind that.
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½ll be flash little Maggie,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And buckjumping Pat.
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½ll be Stringy bark Joe,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And Green-hide Mike.
آ آ آ آ Yes, my Colonials, just
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ As many as you like.

آ آ آ آ Now weï؟½ll stop all immigration,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ We wonï؟½t need it any more;
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ll be having young natives,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Twins by the score.
آ آ آ آ And I wonder what the devil
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Jack Robertson would say
آ آ آ آ If he saw us promenading
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Round the old bullock dray.

آ آ آ آ Oh! itï؟½s time I had an answer,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ If thereï؟½s one to be had,
آ آ آ آ I wouldnï؟½t treat that steer
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In the body half as bad;
آ آ آ آ But he takes as much notice
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Of me, upon my soul,
آ آ آ آ As that old blue stag
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Off-side in the pole.

آ آ آ آ Oh! to tell a lot of lies,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ You know, it is a sin,
آ آ آ آ But Iï؟½ll go up country
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And marry a black gin.
آ آ آ آ Oh! ï؟½Baal gammon white feller,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ This is what sheï؟½ll say,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Budgery you
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And your old bullock dray.ï؟½
meaning:
This evocative piece by Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton), titled "The Old Bullock Dray", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
